content:
SCOPE OF JOB FOR SERVICE CONTRACT FOR VDU HEATER REVAMP
AT BPCL MUMBAI REFINERY
1.0 INTRODUCTION
This Job Specification along with its attachments broadly describes in detail the scope
of supply & work of the Contractor, execution methodology, applicable drawings
(existing and new) and standards / specifications applicable. for the revamp of the
existing CDU3 VDU Heater at BPCL MR
2.0 BRIEF DESCRIPTION OF THE JOB
BPCL-MR intends to revamp the Vacuum Heater (VDU) with Tag No. 131-F-102 as
per the Study Report (Doc No. B810-131-80-44-TR-01).
The revamp is intended to improve the reliability of the heater tube supports. MOC of
the new support system is upgraded to better metallurgy 25Cr-35Ni Nb (existing 25Cr-
20Ni) which is more suited to severe service conditions. Cross section of the new single
ladder side bar is changed from rectangular to circular to add lateral stiffness against
existing ladder deformations.
Introduction of additional coils in the heater with 4 Nos. of additional new tubes (2 rows
of 8” tubes & 2 rows of 6” tube) to be accommodated in the area created by removal
of intermediate cast beam which will add to the increased surface area within same
radiant box and also additional coil in the Convection section.
The Contractor's scope includes Project management, execution, residual
engineering, detailing, fabrication drawing preparation, procurement, pre-fabrication /
fabrication (including supply of materials except for Free issue materials as covered in
tender, consumables / items, as required), inspection, assembly, site erection and
testing assistance during pre-commissioning & commissioning etc. for the following
Fired Heater.
Item No Description Type DUTY MOC of Coil
MMKCal/Hr
131-F-102 VDU HEATER Horizontal tube 34.28 A312 TP316H,
(Revamp) double fired A312 TP316L
box
3.0 DESCRIPTION OF THE EQUIPMENT
The VDU Heater (131-F-102) of CDU3 unit of BPCL MR was designed by M/s EIL and
executed by M/s BHPV & was commissioned in 2005. This heater is a radiant
convective heater with outboard cast + glass APH. Radiant section of the heater
consists of 4 cells and the flue gases leaving the radiant section enters the common
convection section. The convection section consists of both hydrocarbon coils and LP
steam superheating coils. The combustion air to the heater is supplied by two forced
draft fans and is preheated in the air preheater, before entering the combination fired
burners.
breadcrumb: VDU HEATER REVAMPAT BPCL MUMBAI REFINERY > SCOPE OF JOB FOR SERVICE CONTRACT FOR VDU HEATER REVAMPAT BPCL MUMBAI REFINERY

content:
The heater has four Cells with double fired horizontal radiant tubes. Each radiant cell
presently has 17 rows of tubes which are supported along the length at four locations
through ladder type supports. First three rows from radiant outlet (Near top of radiant
section) having size 16”,12” & 8” respectively are supported by four pairs of cast ladder
supports through arch with lug- clevis arrangement. Rest of 14 tube rows of 6” size are
supported through another four pairs of cast ladders in lower radiant section. The 2nd
set of ladders are hanging through lug-clevis type arrangement from an intermediate
cast beam within the radiant box. Both set of ladders are free to move downward and
guided at their bottom. Ladders have rectangular cross section in which, cross-bars
support the radiant coils at predetermined pitch. Material of existing cast ladder
supports is 25Cr-20Ni (A351 Gr HK40).
Along with four radiant cells there is a common single convection section above arch
(offset from radiant cell & centrally placed). This is a balanced draft heater. There are
total 4 passes (one per radiant cell). Each pass radiant inlet is entering near radiant
floor level from tube removal side and exiting at arch level from the terminal side. The
long crossovers from convection to radiant are supported through constant spring
hangers.
4.0 SITE LOCATION
4.1 SITE LOCATION for the subject contract shall be VDU Heater Area in CDU3 Complex,
at BPCL Mumbai Refinery, Mahul, Mumbai 400071
4.2 The prospective Contractors shall make a site visit to familiarize themselves with the
following before submitting the bids:
a) Details of the existing heater system and the space available
b) Site facilities
c) Approach roads / routes
d) Storage facilities
e) Movement constraints and restrictions
f) Area for crane placement / hard stand
g) To understand and plan the dismantling of existing radiant coil support system,
convection coil system, cast beams, prefabrication, modularization scheme,
sequence of pre fabrication & erection and plan adequate mobilization during pre-
shut down / shut down time.
h) To understand and identify erection enabling activities associated with the
execution of work defined in tender document
i) Local / Owner safety & statutory requirements (in regard to barricading, local
isolation with safety precautions for hot work in running refinery)
j) Safety of all personnel and equipment
k) To plan all other activities that are not highlighted but are essential in order to
execute the total work completion as defined in the tender in the limited shut down
duration
4.3 Contractor is required to execute multiple works parallelly in same area considering
short shut down period. Hence, Contractor to plan execution methodology in
consultation with Owner.
4.4 Contractor shall consider site conditions, approach roads, cable trench, drains, space
availability, utility lines battery limit and all issues enabling the erection of the items
covered in the Scope of work. Clarifications, if any, shall be obtained before submitting
his bids. No such issue will be entertained during the execution stage for
compensations. Contractor shall be asked to give detailed presentation on modular
breadcrumb: VDU HEATER REVAMPAT BPCL MUMBAI REFINERY > 4.0 SITE LOCATION

content:
construction / execution methodology before / after award of job and all Owner
comments shall be incorporated.
4.5 Open Space for site work shall be provided by Client within refinery premises for
storage and module / panel assembly purposes. All pre-fabricated items / supplies
shall be brought in refinery premises in weather proof protection packing considering
monsoon. Tarpaulin wrapping shall be done for refractory lined modules / panels.
Temporary blinding shall be done for openings to prevent ingress of water etc. Any
Hydra, trailer & crane etc. for movement / erection of modules etc. to heater location
shall be in Contractors scope if not specifically mentioned in commercial section.
5.0 BASIS OF DESIGN
5.1 The current heater revamp is based on Study Report (Doc No. B810-131-80-44-TR-
01) and list of attachment of tender index (Doc. No. B810-131-80-44-SP-02) along with
the standards and specification and drawings thereof.
5.2 Bidders, after studying the contents of this package may decide to obtain clarifications
before making their offer. Any ambiguity shall be discussed at the pre-bid stage and
once having done so, the offer shall be in strict compliance to the requirements and
technical specifications of this package.
5.3 Various technical specifications / drawings (existing and revamp) / documents as
referred to in this specification and the List of Contents form part of this tender. Any
deviation / exception listed in the bid will be disregarded and treated as null and void.
Any deviation, if listed, will lead to technical rejection without any further reference to
bidder .
5.4 Bidder shall note that as this is a NO DEVIATION tender, therefore no technical details
are required to be submitted in the offer and by the act of submission of bid, it is
assumed & implied that Bidder’s offer is in perfect compliance to the complete bidding
document. Bidders should not bid if they have any deviation against the tender .
5.5 In the event of conflict among the various documents attached with the tender
document, following order of precedence shall apply. However, as a general rule, more
stringent requirement shall govern:
a) Statutory / Owner’s Requirements.
b) Heater GADs, Datasheets and P&IDs attached with the tender.
c) This specification and other job specifications referred herein.
d) EIL standards & Standard specifications.
e) Codes and Standards.
However, all such conflicts shall be referred to OWNER for clarification at the earliest
and the decision of OWNER shall be final and binding on the Contractor without any
cost and delivery implications
5.6 Except as specified herein, all the equipment in the package shall be designed,
manufactured, tested and supplied in accordance with ASME & API standards, data
sheets, specifications and applicable codes and standards.
5.7 It shall be entirely the vendor’s responsibility to ensure compliance to codes and
standards specified for design, fabrication, testing etc. and to all requirements of the
requisition including its specifications and drawings after the order is placed
breadcrumb: VDU HEATER REVAMPAT BPCL MUMBAI REFINERY > 5.0 BASIS OF DESIGN

content:
6.0 SCOPE OF WORK AND TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
6.1 EQUIPMENT SUMMARY
6.1.1 Existing Heater System
The existing heater (131-F-102) is a Double fired box type heater with APH system.
There are four cells in radiant section with common convection along with top mounted
stack.
6.1.2 Revamp Modifications
In brief the purpose of the revamp is: -
a) Installation of new radiant coil supporting system after dismantling of existing cast
beam & ladder type supporting system.
b) Addition of new four tubes / cell in radiant section due to space created after
dismantling of cast beams and safe disposal
c) Bringing of radiant bends within firebox by extension of radiant end walls.
d) Erection of new coils in the provision of future rows in convection section and
modification associated with convection HC inlet transfer line & it’s support.
e) Modification in structural members, dismantling of radiant ETS & Header box,
modification in crossover supporting, Burner / utility piping as per GA drawings.
f) Replacement of all the burner assembly (56 nos) with newly procured burners ( 56
nos).
g) Package contractor is responsible on Single point basis for completing all activities
defined in tender for the revamp of the heater
6.1.3 Heater Repair jobs
a) Replacement of all radiant coils & all hydrocarbon convection coils.
b) Additional Refractory jobs as per the inspection work list, tentative jobs and
quantities as mentioned below:
6.2 GENERAL SCOPE
6.2.1 The following activities are required to be carried out by the Contractor in-house.
Subcontracting of these activities is not allowed:
a) Project management of execution of Heater revamp works
b) Procurement as applicable
c) Construction supervision at shop / site
d) Quality assurance
6.2.2 Time is the essence of this project . Revamp of the existing heater as defined in the
subsequent clauses is to be completed in a limited period shut down time. Contractor
shall accordingly consider maximum pre fabrication in modular / panel forms permitted
as per ODC limits and ensure mobilization of resources – i.e., materials in Contractor’s
scope of supply, manpower and tools & tackles. Also, activities shall be carried out in
parallel to strictly adhere to the shutdown duration.
6.2.3 To facilitate site erection, the fabrication shall be done in modules / panels &
assemblies prefabricated in the Vendor’s shop in line with approved modularization
scheme. The erection modus operandi to be adopted by the Contractor shall be
breadcrumb: VDU HEATER REVAMPAT BPCL MUMBAI REFINERY > 6.0 SCOPE OF WORK AND TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS > 6.2 GENERAL SCOPE

content:
highlighted in the offer in detail along with bar charts. All balance activities are to be
finished in the shutdown period specified.
6.2.4 Contractor to note basic clash check based on available information & drawings has
been done while preparing the Revamp GA drawings. Contractor must perform a
thorough check for feasibility of layout for any clashes / fouling of new revamp system
with existing equipment / Pipe rack / platform / structural members / cables /
instruments /utility lines etc. and consider these modifications as part of the scope with
information to Owner. Any supply / activities required on account of this are part of
Contractor scope.
6.2.5 Residual detailing on account of coil / structural part erection and calculations to ensure
safe lifting, transit and erection at site is in Contractor scope. This should be applicable
for the items to be dismantled and re-erected as per scope requirement.
6.2.6 All existing instruments coming on existing radiant casing / header box and convection
zone which are required to be dismantled should be taken out carefully during shut
down and kept safely for re-installation on the new modules / panels
6.2.7 Rerouting of utility and process piping to enable modification work (cutting, fabrication,
supporting, testing, insulation installation) is in contractor scope.
6.2.8 Contractor shall understand and verify at site before proceeding wrt the structural
sections and dimensions at the major interface locations with existing structure,
platforms, ladders, utility and process piping etc
6.2.9 External insulation installation, Ceramic Fibre Blanket (CFB) etc. including material
required for completion of the revamp of Heater system as per the tender attachments
are in Contractor scope. In case of any ambiguity clarification to be obtained by the
Contractor at Pre bid stage. No clarification / cost implication on this account shall be
entertained at post award stage and decision of Owner shall be final.
6.2.10 Restoration, preservation and protection of existing equipment and their accessories
shall be in Contractor’s scope of work. In case of any damage during, the same shall
be rectified and reinstalled and made in working order to the satisfaction of Engineer-
incharge.
6.2.11 For complete scope of work and supply, fabrication, erection, pre-commissioning /
commissioning spares shall be worked out by the Contractor and shall be included
within the quoted Lump sum price.
6.2.12 For details of facilities / utilities if any, (e.g., construction power, water, storage space
etc.) contractor to refer commercial portion of tender.
6.2.13 To ensure safe and suitable working conditions during activities inside the heater, the
contractor shall provide technical cooling system (portable HVAC) of adequate
capacity and quantity ( min 02 nos) to maintain a comfortable and safe temperature for
personnels working inside heater.
6.3 DETAILED SCOPE OF JOB
breadcrumb: VDU HEATER REVAMPAT BPCL MUMBAI REFINERY > 6.0 SCOPE OF WORK AND TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS > 6.2 GENERAL SCOPE

content:
6.3.1 Contractor to refer this specification along with the Study Report (Doc No. B810-131-
80-44-TR-01) and list of attachment of tender index (Doc. No. B810-131-80-44-SP-02)
for understanding the complete scope and requirements of the project.
6.3.2 Contractor to note that Study report and drawings were prepared to cover execution of
work as per BPCL preference. Contractor to refer report & revamp GA drawings
thoroughly and queries if any shall be sorted before proceeding further. Detailed
execution philosophy for pre shut down and shut down activities need to be submitted
by contractor to Owner for review and approval.
6.3.3 Any item not specifically stated but required for completion of the job is considered in
Contractor scope. It’s contractor’s responsibility to identify and supply any & all other
items required as per the scope of the revamp job and to make it complete in all
aspects. No other items shall be provided by Owner. Major supply items are described
below for contractor’s reference:
6.3.4 Contractor to refer revamp GA drawings for understanding of sequence of activities.
Balance technical details if any as required for fabrication & detailing are in Contractor
scope and shall be developed by the Contractor and submitted for Owner review.
6.3.5 Free Issue Material
a) BPCL MR shall procure and issue at site storage area following items of the heater
system for Contractor for pre shut down work and their erection during pre-shut
down / shut down as per the execution philosophy.
b) No other items shall be issued as free issue by Owner and all balance items i.e.
including refractory & external insulation, structural steel, structural plates for end
wall extension on east & west side of the heater & its refractory, barricading
material, ceramic fibre blankets / module, platform grating, handrail, sight doors
etc. consumables, gaskets (non- asbestos suitable to service temperature), bolts
for companion flanges, anchor bolts for equipment erection and interface related
items need to be taken care in his scope by contractor to make the revamp job
complete.
c) Considering revamp job, Contractor shall identify all activities & items which shall
be required for execution / completeness and should be ready with back up
resources / manpower for meeting last minute gaps reported if any. Contractor shall
obtain clarification (if any) prior to bidding.
d) Free issue items:
i. New Cast ladder supports for radiant tubes
ii. HC Pressure parts only (Radiant tubes, Convection tubes and related piping
fittings)
iii. Refractory Material required for Additional Refractory jobs as per the
inspection work list. (List attached)
e) For utility supply (electricity, water etc.) by Owner, Contractor shall refer
commercial section of the tender. No utility material will be supplied by BPCL.
f) All materials except FIMs is in contractor’s scope
6.3.6 Heater Revamp Jobs as per EIL Study
Procurement, fabrication, erection / installation of the following as per GA drawing:
breadcrumb: VDU HEATER REVAMPAT BPCL MUMBAI REFINERY > 6.0 SCOPE OF WORK AND TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS > 6.2 GENERAL SCOPE > 6.3.6 Heater Revamp Jobs as per EIL Study

content:
 Radiant Zone
a) Dismantling of structures on radiant walls (East, west side)
b) Dismantling of Radiant Walls
c) Dismantling of existing ladder supports including intermediate cast beams
d) Erection of new ladder support
e) Fabrication and installation of 6” and 8” radiant coils (free issue) in the space
created and associated NDTs.
f) Supply and fabrication of new extended Radiant fire box to bring the radiant
coil bends inside radiant section (as per the drawing).
g) Supply and fabrication of structures and platforms suiting the requirement of
new radiant wall.
h) Counterweights / attachment mechanism for cross over supporting.
i) Sleeve and sealing cloth provision as per revamp GAD in extended radiant
casing at coil entry and exit and SI connection location.
j) Ladder support Bottom guide / sleeves / tapered “L” clamps as per revamp
drawing.
k) New structural members required at arch for the supporting of ladder & coils.
l) Refractory works
a. Procurement and installation of all refractory and insulation materials
like ceramic fiber blanket / modules vacuum formed blocks, castable,
mortars, rock-wool, mineral wool, SS foil etc. as per drawings,
standards and specifications required for heater modification activities
as mentioned above.
b. Procurement and installation of all types and shapes of tie back
materials (SS 316 / 316L) like V/Y lugs, retainer plates, shelf flats, chain
link, speed fix washers, etc required for heater modification activities as
mentioned above.
c. Miscellaneous materials like 0.1 mm thick SS 316 foil, tape, rope and
fibre, heat resistant adhesive, tar paper, mortars, ceramic fibre for gap
packing, etc. required for execution and completion of the job required
for heater modification activities as mentioned above.
d. Repair and patch works of all the damaged refractory because of
change of ladder support and burner replacement activities including
material procurement.
 Convection Zone
a) Fabrication and erection of Convection coils (coils free issue) in the space
provided for future rows and connection with existing coil with the help of new
jump overs as per revamp drawing including associated NDTs.
b) Convection Header box / sleeves with sealing cloth to be modified to
accommodate the changes at jump over and openings due to shifting of HC
inlet terminal location.
6.3.7 Heater Jobs as per inspection recommendations
Fabrication, erection / installation of the following as per GA drawing:
 Radiant Zone
breadcrumb: VDU HEATER REVAMPAT BPCL MUMBAI REFINERY > 6.0 SCOPE OF WORK AND TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS > 6.2 GENERAL SCOPE > 6.3.7 Heater Jobs as per inspection recommendations

content:
a) Replacement of Radiant coils as per the inspection recommendations
b) Refractory works as per the inspection recommendations. (List Attached)
c) Hydrotesting of the complete process coil circuit
 Convection Zone
a) Replacement of Complete convection coils as per the inspection
recommendations.
b) Fabrication, erection & supporting of convection HC inlet terminal piping (pipe
free issue) as per site condition.
c) Refractory works as per the inspection recommendations. (List Attached)
d) Hydrotesting of the complete process coil circuit
6.3.8 Miscellaneous Jobs
a) There is one line item for various SOR, the vendor must quote accordingly.
b) SOR items shall be operated for the jobs which are being executed outside the
heater and for the jobs being done apart from the scope as mentioned above.
c) Heater burner replacement and associated piping modifications (if any) will be
done as per the rates given in SOR.
d) All the process piping modifications and associated NDTs coming outside the
heater shall be operated under SOR.
6.3.9 Post Shutdown Activities
Any balance work from pre-shutdown and shutdown activities due to any unforeseen
situation, to be executed by contractor post shutdown without any extra cost.
Contractor to ensure completion of all activities during pre-shutdown and shutdown
period
6.4 SCOPE OF SITE WORK AND EXECUTION
6.4.1 Detailed execution philosophy to be prepared for parallel work in all 4 cells of radiant
section and convection section to complete replacement within shutdown with
adequate manpower / resources. Compliance to BPCL safety requirements / hot work
permits need to be ensured by contractor. Pre-fabrication during pre-shut down to be
maximized to save shut down time.
6.4.2 A detailed site activities schedule along with a Field Quality Assurance Plan (FQAP)
shall be prepared by vendor at least 30 days prior to the mobilization of the resources
for site work and the same shall be approved by BPCL before the start of site activities.
Further to site mobilization, daily progress monitoring of the job shall be done jointly
by the vendor and the Engineer-In-Charge. Site progress activity report shall be
circulated to BPCL representatives by the vendor on weekly basis. Final report of site
activities shall be submitted by the vendor to BPCL after the completion of site activities
and before the start of commissioning.
6.4.3 Vendor shall establish a project site office consisting of the following functions:
S. Minimum
DESCRIPTION
NO. Personnel required
1. RCM / Site-In-Charge 1
2. Lead Discipline
breadcrumb: VDU HEATER REVAMPAT BPCL MUMBAI REFINERY > 6.0 SCOPE OF WORK AND TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS > S. Minimum

content:
Piping 6
Lead QA/QC Engineer 2
Lead Planning Engineer 1
Lead HSE Officer 6
3. Discipline Engineers + Supervisors
Civil/Structural 2
Piping 4
Painting/insulation 2
Safety Engineers / Supervisors 6
Scaffolding Inspector 4
Document Controller 1
Administrative Officer 1
Electrician 2
Refractory Inspector ( API 936/ 982) 2
Deployment of skilled/unskilled/semiskilled workers (welders, riggers, fabricators,
painters, helpers, etc) shall be as per requirement subject to approval from Engineer
In Charge.
6.4.3.1 Role of TPIA / Qualified refractory inspector (to be engaged by the contractor)
a) Irrespective of the checks performed by BPCL inspection team, the TPIA /
contractor's refractory inspector shall inspect the lining at all identified stages as per
approved ITP/ Check points and maintain inspection formats.
b) Inspection of refractory works shall be performed by contractor's refractory inspector
(API 936/ 982 certified inspector for refractory jobs) and a proper inspection report
with photographs duly signed / stamped by the inspector shall be generated and
submitted to BPCL. Two nos of inspector shall be deployed during turnaround
working in 12hours shift (Morning & Night shift ) and co-ordination with BPCL
inspection team for technical and quality supervision.
6.4.4 Burners
All 56 existing burner assemblies shall be replaced with new ones. The associated
piping connected to the burners shall be reinstalled, and any required modifications
related to piping will be carried out simultaneously during this activity.
6.4.5 PMI
a) Material identification shall be done at site as per PMI specification listed in Tender.
b) The quality and inspection plan of the Contractor shall comply with all the requirements
in the tender
c) PMI of welded joints to be carried out during pre-shutdown and shutdown period.
6.4.6 HYDROTESTING
breadcrumb: VDU HEATER REVAMPAT BPCL MUMBAI REFINERY > 6.0 SCOPE OF WORK AND TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS > S. Minimum > 6.4.6 HYDROTESTING

content:
a) Apart from final hydro testing of assembled radiant and convection tubes, each
convection studded tubes shall be hydro tested at ground after welding of bridging
pieces, 50 mm extra length may be considered on each side of tube for hydro testing
which shall be cut to final length as per drawing. Complete water removal after hydro
testing shall be done from heater coils.
b) Hydrotest to be carried out with DM water.
6.4.7 PRESERVATION
All material supplies and bought-out equipment shall be supplied with proper wrapping and
packing, so that they will be preserved in as new condition during handling and under the
rainy/ corrosive outdoor condition of storage for a minimum period of 6 months. Contractor
shall submit equipment manuals/ data folder which shall contain storage/ preservation,
handling, erection instructions
7.0 BATTERY LIMIT
7.1 Vendor’s battery limit shall be the limited to the works on the heater radiant and
convection section and all jobs associated with the same.
7.2 All jobs on the radiant and convection coil till the heater inlet and outlet battery limit
7.3 Hot air duct to the burner beneath the heater and requiring work as part of the burner
replacement is part of this tender.
7.4 All platforms and structures outside the radiant box and convection section in the
respective elevation needing modification as part of the heater revamp.
7.5 Heater Stack and Flue gas ducting is not part of the scope in this tender
8.0 INSPECTION AND TESTING
8.1 The Contractor is expected to have a strong Quality Assurance system active both at office
and at site. The quality requirements, documentation requirements, formats, protocols,
procedures defined in the Tender specifications shall be followed by the Contractor.
8.2 Contractor is also required to follow the specifications: 6-78-0001, 6-78-0002 attached
elsewhere in the tender.
8.3 The specification 6-78-0003 shall also be applicable for procurement of items in
Contractor’s scope
8.4 Vendor shall prepare Quality Assurance Plan (QAP) and get it approved by BPCL-MR
before starting any activity. Inspection shall be done as per BPCL – MR approved QAP
plan.
8.5 Inspection shall be carried out both before fabrication and during fabrication and also
for sub-ordered materials, if any as per the approved plan.
8.6 Inspection as per the QAP will be carried out by the vendor representative, BPCL-MR
or BPCL-MR approved third party inspection (TPI) agency.
8.7 Inspection within refinery premises to be carried out by BPCL inspection team. For outside
refinery, list of BPCL nominated TPIA inspection agencies is provided in commercial
section. Contractor needs to coordinate with BPCL inspection team or BPCL appointed
TPIA as may be instructed by BPCL
8.8 The Third Party Inspection agency (TPIA) engaged for inspection, review and issue of
IRN before dispatch shall be BPCL-MR approved TPIA only.
8.9 Rejection
8.9.1 Structure or parts thereof and materials entering therein, indicating irremediable or
injurious defects, improper fabrication, excessive repairs or that are not in accordance with
breadcrumb: VDU HEATER REVAMPAT BPCL MUMBAI REFINERY > 8.0 INSPECTION AND TESTING > 8.9 Rejection

content:
this specification shall be subject to rejection. They shall also be subject to rejection if such
conditions are discovered after acceptance at the fabricator's works or at site.
8.9.2 Completed equipment, parts of equipment, or materials containing defects originating with
the supplier's materials, or workmanship or that are not in complete compliance with the
requirements of the order will be subject to rejection.
8.9.3 Discovery of conditions warranting rejection, after inspection and acceptance of the
equipment by the Purchaser does not relieve the supplier of the responsibility to comply
with the order
9.0 GUARANTEE
9.1 Contractor shall guarantee revamped portion of heater components / parts against
defective design and materials, poor workmanship and failure from normal usage for a
period as specified in tender document / GPC. Supplier shall repair or replace, without
charge any defective parts found within that time. This guarantee shall also include
subordered/ subcontracted / bought-out items forming part of the total contractor supplies.If
a separate guarantee clause has been specified in procurement specification for items in
Contractor scope of supply, then the stringent of the two shall govern.
9.2 Contractor shall guarantee to accept full responsibility for all fabricated components / parts
of the revamped portion of heater system in Contractor's scope of supply / fabricated by
him in regard to selection, fabrication and coordination of the various components.
9.3 Any damage caused to existing heaters / FIMs during execution of the job by Contractor
shall be rectified without no cost or time implication to Client.
10.0 FABRICATION & ERECTION SPARES
10.1 Fabrication / erection / pre-commissioning / commissioning spares (excluding mandatory
spares) including material for welder’s qualification shall be worked out by the Bidders and
shall be included as quantities additional to the actual requirements. The price of these
shall be included in the total lumpsum price.
10.2 These spares are not required to be deposited to the OWNER’s stores, but should be
available with Contractor at site as and when required, to avoid delays during site
execution.
10.3 Any leftover items will not be taken by the OWNER and site shall be cleared of all such
items at the completion of the job.
10.4 Contractor to note that adequate commissioning and erection spares & consumables need
to be considered in his scope of supply. Also, any dismantled & temporary items which are
not required in final system shall be cleared from the site and disposed safely at designated
place as per Statutory / Owner requirements (refer elsewhere). Site shall be cleared of
such temporary material and construction debris
11.0 COMMISSIOINING
11.1 Vendor shall commence the pre-commissioning activities for systems and equipment
after successful installation/erection as per the BPCL approved checklists and plans.
11.2 Assistance as required during check listing of revamped heater system, precommissioning
and commissioning shall be provided by the Contractor and shall be included in the lump
sum price .
11.3 Housekeeping shall be carried out at fabrication yard and heater area during the pre-
shutdown and shutdown periods after completion of the work.
12.0 APPLICABLE CODES AND STANDARDS, DRAWINGS & SPECIFICATIONS

14.0 DOCUMENTATION
14.1 Vendor shall submit documentations viz. GAD, P&IDs, QAP/ITPs, detailed project
schedule, Schedule for job execution during the turnaround (hourly basis), data sheets
and all other essential documents as applicable progressively after the award of PO
and also as part of project completion in the form of As Built Documentation.
14.2 Vendor shall essentially submit the documents in hard copy (3 copies) as well as soft
copy.
15.0 EVALUATION CRITERIA:
The bidders shall be evaluated technically based on the technical specifications,
Documents submitted along with Bid for evaluation and conditions mentioned in the scope
of supply/work.
The bidders shall be evaluated on overall lowest quote (L1) basis.
AS this is NIL deviation tender, Bids submitted without/insufficient documents mentioned
above for bid evaluation are liable for rejection without any further correspondence in this
regard. Bidder are requested to furnish all the documents along with Bid. Also, all the
queries shall be cleared prior to submitting proposal and offers with deviation are liable
for rejection without any further correspondence.
16.0 PAINTING AND SURFACE PREPARATION
Vendor to refer (AES-S-5430) for the instructions regarding painting and surface
preparation, wherever applicable.
16.0 PACKING AND TRANSPORTATION
16.1 Transportation of equipment to site shall be in a single/multiple piece as per
convenience and the defined erection plan, in ready to install condition. Lifting lugs /
arrangement to be provided on skids considering safety and ease of erection.
16.2 After completion of all testing and inspection, the inside of all equipment/piping shall
be thoroughly drained and dried out. Equipment shall be completely dried by passing
hot air for enough time until no further increase in relative humidity of outgoing air is
observed.
16.3 All spare parts furnished by vendor shall be wrapped and packaged so that they will
be preserved in original as per new conditions under the normal conditions of the
storage prevailing in India and shall be properly tagged and coded so that later
identification as to intended equipment usage will be facilitated. They shall be packed
separately, clearly marked as “spare parts” and to be shipped at the same time as the
equipment.
breadcrumb: VDU HEATER REVAMPAT BPCL MUMBAI REFINERY > 16.0 PACKING AND TRANSPORTATION

content:
16.4 Packing list shall be furnished so that the parts can be handled without uncarting, if
desired.
16.5 Vendor shall furnish preservation procedure taking care of all stages (i.e. pre-
installation, erection & commissioning). Packing shall be suitable for at least six (6)
months of storage at site.
16.6 Any damage during shipment shall be taken care by the vendor with fresh material at
its own cost.
16.7 Equipment shall be properly wrapped and packaged to effect protection against
corrosion or degradation during shipping and storage at site.
16.8 All parts shall be provided with match and identification marks and also bear the
equipment tag number.
17.0 WARRANTY/DEFECT LIABILTY
17.1 The supplier shall have final and total responsibility for the design, material,
manufacturing, workmanship, and performance of the revamped heater including all
equipment’s and associated accessories supplied by the vendor and shall provide
warranty against defective design, material and workmanship for a duration of 12
months from the date of commissioning of heater at BPCL MR or 18 months from the
date of receipt of the latest material in the package, whichever is earlier.
17.2 Any defects observed during the warranty period shall be rectified by the vendor in a
time-bound manner at their own cost. Vendor shall submit Performance Bank
Guarantee for the contract, which shall be at 5% of the basic value of the contract valid
from the date of receipt of all the supply components of the heater package at site and
up to 12 months from the date of successful commissioning of the heater plus 6 months
claim period.
18.0 HSE
18.1 HSE is the primary commitment of BPCL. Design studies, material supply and
construction activities must be delivered to comply with all HSE aspects of the project.
Vendor shall demonstrate his own HSE commitment.
18.2 Vendor shall deploy adequate safety officers during the pre-shutdown and shutdown
periods to ensure enhanced safety compliance.
18.3 Vendor must ensure that the design, fabrication and testing of his equipment doesn’t
endanger the health and security of his own employees and future operators of the
plant. In addition, all possible efforts must be made to mx1inimize the environmental
impact of his activities.
18.4 All required precautions for the work to proceed safely without interruption (health,
safety, and environment) during site erection must be taken into account.
18.5 Vendor shall provide any necessary recommendation for installation in order to ensure
a safe erection and operation of his equipment.
18.6 Since during pre-shut down (PSD) the revamp activities are to be taken up in a running
plant, all necessary barricading, safety related precautions, tools & tackles shall be the
Contractor’s responsibility. All the OWNER’s safety recommendations, HSE requirements
and all applicable rules & regulations shall be complied to by the Contractor.
18.7 Contractor to note that during Pre shut down hot work shall be permitted with compliance
of safety requirements / local barricading isolation. Owner’s approval for the same shall be
obtained before proceeding for hot work
breadcrumb: VDU HEATER REVAMPAT BPCL MUMBAI REFINERY > 18.0 HSE

content:
18.8 All the vendor employees deputed at site, including supervisors, are required to attend
safety training in BPCL failing which they will not be accepted as supervisors
irrespective of their academic qualifications and work experience. All safety
precautions as per permit conditions and as per instructions from site in charge shall
be abided by the contractor.
18.9 It shall be the sole responsibility of the vendor for safe and proper execution of the
work. Specifying any procedure or approving any procedure by BPCL shall not relieve
the contractor from his responsibility towards fulfilling job objective.
18.10 All the BPCL's fire & safety rules and regulations or any other framed from time to time
are essentially to be followed and the vendor is liable for any lapses and penal
deduction from the bills shall be levied as per BPCL's safety financial deterrents.
18.11 The vendor shall compulsorily follow all the requirements/instructions of BPCL's work
authorization & permit systems and gate entry procedure for labour, staff & material as
per the specific procedures & regulations.
19.0 BID PRESENTATION
19.1 The bid shall include the following technical information along with the offer:
a) Job execution philosophy including pre shutdown and shutdown activities and the
time schedule,
b) Details of the crane deployment and the site management.
c) Duly filled concession/deviation (as per attached) form concerning deviation and
exceptions if any
d) Fabrication/Delivery schedule
e) Transportation philosophy
f) List of “Main Material” Sub-Vendors
g) Supplier HSE commitment
h) List of startup & commissioning spares
i) Third party inspection agency
j) List of references (Past track record as required for BQC)
m) Other documents as listed in vendor data requirement.
20.0 DELIVERY / COMPLETION PERIOD
The Delivery/ Completion period is defined as follows:
20.1 The start date of the job will be the PO date and the PO will be valid for one year period.
20.2 The subject heater revamp is planned during the turnaround of CDU3 unit in the month
of April 2026 with an expected start date of 16 th April 2026.
20.3 The shutdown duration will be for 30 days with available engineering days of 22 for the
heater revamp job. The engineering days will govern as the time of completion for this
contract.
20.4 The pre-shutdown activity of this job is planned to start from 20 th of Feb 2026 and the
contractor should mobilize the required manpower and resources for the same.
20.5 All the material in the scope of supply of the contractor should reach the worksite latest
by 15 th Feb 2026.

COMPLETION
Price reduction due to delay/slippage in delivery/completion shall be applicable at the
rate of 1 % (One percentage) of total contract cost for every day of delay or part thereof
subject to maximum of 5% (Five percentage) of total contract cost
22.0 PAYMENT TERMS
22.1 For Heater Revamp Job as per the Line Item

b. 20% against completion of Pre TA activities.

unit.

b. 50% against completion of installation during TA

unit.
d. 5% against submission of documents
22.3 For Heater Convection Tube Replacement Job as per the Line Item
a. 30% against completion of Pre TA activities.
b. 50% against completion of installation during TA
c. 15% against successful completion of the heater revamp jobs and startup of the
unit.
d. 5% against submission of documents
22.4 For other SOR items in the contract payment against submission of the RA Bills as per

22.5 Payment will be on a pro-rata basis as per the mutually agreed billing schedule by
vendor & BPCL prepared after the award of PO.

24.0 PRE-BID MEETING & SITE VISIT
24.1 It is mandatory for each bidder/contractor to attend the pre-bid meeting to get clear
idea of the scope of work and to clear queries if any.
24.2 The Contractor is advised to visit the site & ascertain the existing conditions of the VDU
heater. The contractor is also advised to get acquainted with site conditions, drawing,
specifications and other documents which form part of contract to be entered into. No
claim shall be entertained later, on the grounds of lack of knowledge .
XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX
6.4.8 Suggested execution steps are listed below
6.4.8.1 RADIANT SECTION
a) Additional beams on arch as shown in the revamp GA drawing (except at new tube
support location i.e. at grid 2 & grid 3) can be welded prior to shut down. This will reduce
the execution time during actual shutdown activity and will facilitate safety of
subsequent activities.
b) The beams just above tube support location should be cut preferably from a single
beam for easy interface matching & assembly later with bolted section as per drawing.
The beam length should also be checked as per actual site measurements for any
variation / tolerance. If required, certain minimum extra length can be considered to
suit the site variations during actual fabrication / installation
c) It is important to note that cutting of any existing arch steel structure is only to be done
once addition of new ladder support structure frame members on arch is completed
d) Existing central two ladder supports (at grid 2 & grid 3) are now to be relocated with
offset of 222 mm from it’s current location. Location of ladder supports near grid 1 & 4
is unchanged. All these arch steel members now have a bolted removable beam
section provision in the modified arrangement. Existing arch plate / stiffeners at this
location to be cut and removed as per revamp drawing.
breadcrumb: VDU HEATER REVAMPAT BPCL MUMBAI REFINERY > 24.0 PRE-BID MEETING & SITE VISIT

content:
e) After cooling of the heater during shutdown, radiant coils shall be cut and removed.
f) Existing Ladder supports (32 nos.) and cast beam (16 nos.) to be dismantled and
removed /safely kept at the designated place.
g) The main structural members and instrument connections (except as shown in the
revamp GA drawing) shall not be dismantled while execution of the work.
h) Balance structural work at arch for tube supporting beam etc. to be completed as per
revamp GA drawing.
i) For the extension of the radiant section and placement of new coils, all existing Radiant
ETS & Header boxes to be completely removed and dismantled in all 4 cells.
j) Safety of the coils from grinder / cutting tool (to avoid any hot work / cut / dent / damage
to the coil) to be ensured while removal of these ETS by using temporary 3mm thick
SS bent half sleeve for placing between coil and ETS. The header box and ETS
dismantling work can be done in parallel through separate gang in all four cells.
k) Insert ladder supports from the arch opening and hang it with structural beam as per
the detail shown in the drawing.
l) Insert the replacement radiant coils in the furnace box and align them with the tube
supports.
m) Cut the radiant jump over as shown in the coil drawing. Insert new 6” and 8” radiant
coils in the space created due to deletion of intermediate cast beam and complete the
welds and NDT.
n) Cross bars below respective tubes to be placed at all locations. Ensure proper resting
of the coils at respective supports.
o) Check the alignment of the coils & ladder support system as per GA drawings.
p) Ensure proper seating of the ladder supports with structural members and gaps
between bottom of ladder supports & floor as per GA drawing.
q) Proper sealing through CF gaskets / tape gasket to be done at casing cut-out locations.
Sealing cloth to be provided at header box entry location as per EIL standard.
r) Remove platforms locally in front of end walls (if required) along grid 1 & grid 4 and re-
instate them after completion of the work.
s) Counterweights to be provided for crossovers at current spring location after
dismantling of existing SP-1 spring support for pass 1 & 4. It should be ensured that
the counter weight remain active & free for complete range of movement due to coil
expansion during operation for entire travel (150 mm) and also should not obstruct the
walkway
t) Radiant fire box long walls to be extended to bring the radiant coil bends inside radiant
section as per the revamp GA drawing.
u) Tube removal door, sight doors, instruments, explosion door to be provided in end walls
for the replacement of coils & ladder supports during shutdown as per the revamp
drawing.
v) New refractory / extended casing structural support frames need to be provided as per
revamp drawing / in-situ after clearing space from utility lines etc. These lies will need
to be re-routed to accommodate slightly bigger radiant section
w) Apply refractory / CF modules at floor / wall / arch where modification is done. CFB
with anchors to be used to cover the voids / gaps created due to removal of existing
tube supports up to the existing refractory thickness
6.4.8.2 CONVECTION SECTION
a) After cooling of the heater during shutdown, HC convection coils shall be cut and
removed
b) The existing convection coils to be replaced with new coils as part of replacement job.
c) New convection coils to be placed in the space provided for future rows and connect
with existing convection coils with the help of new jump overs as per revamp drawing.
d) Convection HC inlet terminal piping modification to be done. Hydrotesting of the
complete process coil circuit to be completed.
breadcrumb: VDU HEATER REVAMPAT BPCL MUMBAI REFINERY > 24.0 PRE-BID MEETING & SITE VISIT

content:
e) Convection Header box / sleeves to be modified to accommodate the changes due to
shifting of terminal location.
f) Insulation of external crossover and jump overs to be completed
6.4.9 Following areas can be considered for parallel work to minimize shut down
a. Inside radiant section dismantling, temporary support and replacement with New
radiant tube installation & welding work
b. Arch area structural steel work
c. Complete ETS / Header box removal & extension of radiant casing plate with its
supporting
d. Convection section coil addition & transfer line modification
e. CPR of welded joints in radiant and convection section.
6.4.10 MODULARIZATION / TRIAL ASSEMBY
Trial assembly of the modules / panels shall be carried out by the Contractor at the
fabrication yard before dispatch of the modules to site. s
6.4.11 REFRACTORY
a) All refractory and Insulation work shall be carried out as per the tender specifications /
standards for Refractory lining.
b) The refractory materials which have a shelf life, should have at least 6 month of
remaining shelf life on arrival at site
6.4.12 Burners
All 56 existing burner assemblies shall be replaced with new ones. The associated
piping connected to the burners shall be reinstalled, and any required modifications
related to piping will be carried out simultaneously during this activity.
6.4.13 PMI
d) Material identification shall be done at site as per PMI specification listed in Tender.
e) The quality and inspection plan of the Contractor shall comply with all the requirements
in the tender
f) PMI of welded joints to be carried out during pre-shutdown and shutdown period.
6.4.14 HYDROTESTING
c) Apart from final hydro testing of assembled radiant and convection tubes, each
convection studded tubes shall be hydro tested at ground after welding of bridging
pieces, 50 mm extra length may be considered on each side of tube for hydro testing
which shall be cut to final length as per drawing. Complete water removal after hydro
testing shall be done from heater coils.
d) Hydrotest to be carried out with DM water.
6.4.15 PRESERVATION
All material supplies and bought-out equipment shall be supplied with proper wrapping and
packing, so that they will be preserved in as new condition during handling and under the
breadcrumb: VDU HEATER REVAMPAT BPCL MUMBAI REFINERY > 24.0 PRE-BID MEETING & SITE VISIT > 6.4.15 PRESERVATION

content:
rainy/ corrosive outdoor condition of storage for a minimum period of 6 months. Contractor
shall submit equipment manuals/ data folder which shall contain storage/ preservation,
handling, erection instructions
11.0 BATTERY LIMIT
11.1 Vendor’s battery limit shall be the limited to the works on the heater radiant and
convection section and all jobs associated with the same.
11.2 All jobs on the radiant and convection coil till the heater inlet and outlet battery limit
11.3 Hot air duct to the burner beneath the heater and requiring work as part of the burner
replacement is part of this tender.
11.4 All platforms and structures outside the radiant box and convection section in the
respective elevation needing modification as part of the heater revamp.
11.5 Heater Stack and Flue gas ducting is not part of the scope in this tender
12.0 INSPECTION AND TESTING
12.1 The Contractor is expected to have a strong Quality Assurance system active both at office
and at site. The quality requirements, documentation requirements, formats, protocols,
procedures defined in the Tender specifications shall be followed by the Contractor.
12.2 Contractor is also required to follow the specifications: 6-78-0001, 6-78-0002 attached
elsewhere in the tender.
12.3 The specification 6-78-0003 shall also be applicable for procurement of items in
Contractor’s scope
12.4 Vendor shall prepare Quality Assurance Plan (QAP) and get it approved by BPCL-MR
before starting any activity. Inspection shall be done as per BPCL – MR approved QAP
plan.
12.5 Inspection shall be carried out both before fabrication and during fabrication and also
for sub-ordered materials, if any as per the approved plan.
12.6 Inspection as per the QAP will be carried out by the vendor representative, BPCL-MR
or BPCL-MR approved third party inspection (TPI) agency.
12.7 Inspection within refinery premises to be carried out by BPCL inspection team. For outside
refinery, list of BPCL nominated TPIA inspection agencies is provided in commercial
section. Contractor needs to coordinate with BPCL inspection team or BPCL appointed
TPIA as may be instructed by BPCL
12.8 The Third Party Inspection agency (TPIA) engaged for inspection, review and issue of
IRN before dispatch shall be BPCL-MR approved TPIA only.
12.9 Rejection
12.9.1 Structure or parts thereof and materials entering therein, indicating irremediable or
injurious defects, improper fabrication, excessive repairs or that are not in accordance with
this specification shall be subject to rejection. They shall also be subject to rejection if such
conditions are discovered after acceptance at the fabricator's works or at site.
12.9.2 Completed equipment, parts of equipment, or materials containing defects originating with
the supplier's materials, or workmanship or that are not in complete compliance with the
requirements of the order will be subject to rejection.
12.9.3 Discovery of conditions warranting rejection, after inspection and acceptance of the
equipment by the Purchaser does not relieve the supplier of the responsibility to comply
with the order

shall be rectified without no cost or time implication to Client.

15.0 DOCUMENTATION
breadcrumb: VDU HEATER REVAMPAT BPCL MUMBAI REFINERY > 15.0 DOCUMENTATION

content:
15.1 Vendor shall submit documentations viz. GAD, P&IDs, QAP/ITPs, detailed project
schedule, Schedule for job execution during the turnaround (hourly basis), data sheets
and all other essential documents as applicable progressively after the award of PO
and also as part of project completion in the form of As Built Documentation.
15.2 Vendor shall essentially submit the documents in hard copy (3 copies) as well as soft
copy.
17.0 EVALUATION CRITERIA:
The bidders shall be evaluated technically based on the technical specifications,
Documents submitted along with Bid for evaluation and conditions mentioned in the scope
of supply/work.
The bidders shall be evaluated on overall lowest quote (L1) basis.
AS this is NIL deviation tender, Bids submitted without/insufficient documents mentioned
above for bid evaluation are liable for rejection without any further correspondence in this
regard. Bidder are requested to furnish all the documents along with Bid. Also, all the
queries shall be cleared prior to submitting proposal and offers with deviation are liable
for rejection without any further correspondence.
18.0 PAINTING AND SURFACE PREPARATION
Vendor to refer (AES-S-5430) for the instructions regarding painting and surface
preparation, wherever applicable.
17.0 PACKING AND TRANSPORTATION
17.1 Transportation of equipment to site shall be in a single/multiple piece as per
convenience and the defined erection plan, in ready to install condition. Lifting lugs /
arrangement to be provided on skids considering safety and ease of erection.
17.2 After completion of all testing and inspection, the inside of all equipment/piping shall
be thoroughly drained and dried out. Equipment shall be completely dried by passing
hot air for enough time until no further increase in relative humidity of outgoing air is
observed.
17.3 All spare parts furnished by vendor shall be wrapped and packaged so that they will
be preserved in original as per new conditions under the normal conditions of the
storage prevailing in India and shall be properly tagged and coded so that later
identification as to intended equipment usage will be facilitated. They shall be packed
separately, clearly marked as “spare parts” and to be shipped at the same time as the
equipment.
17.4 Packing list shall be furnished so that the parts can be handled without uncarting, if
desired.
17.5 Vendor shall furnish preservation procedure taking care of all stages (i.e. pre-
installation, erection & commissioning). Packing shall be suitable for at least six (6)
months of storage at site.
17.6 Any damage during shipment shall be taken care by the vendor with fresh material at
its own cost.
17.7 Equipment shall be properly wrapped and packaged to effect protection against
corrosion or degradation during shipping and storage at site.
17.8 All parts shall be provided with match and identification marks and also bear the
equipment tag number.

content:
18.0 WARRANTY/DEFECT LIABILTY
18.1 The supplier shall have final and total responsibility for the design, material,
manufacturing, workmanship, and performance of the revamped heater including all
equipment’s and associated accessories supplied by the vendor and shall provide
warranty against defective design, material and workmanship for a duration of 12
months from the date of commissioning of heater at BPCL MR or 18 months from the
date of receipt of the latest material in the package, whichever is earlier.
18.2 Any defects observed during the warranty period shall be rectified by the vendor in a
time-bound manner at their own cost. Vendor shall submit Performance Bank
Guarantee for the contract, which shall be at 5% of the basic value of the contract valid
from the date of receipt of all the supply components of the heater package at site and
up to 12 months from the date of successful commissioning of the heater plus 6 months
claim period.
19.0 HSE
19.1 HSE is the primary commitment of BPCL. Design studies, material supply and
construction activities must be delivered to comply with all HSE aspects of the project.
Vendor shall demonstrate his own HSE commitment.
19.2 Vendor shall deploy adequate safety officers during the pre-shutdown and shutdown
periods to ensure enhanced safety compliance.
19.3 Vendor must ensure that the design, fabrication and testing of his equipment doesn’t
endanger the health and security of his own employees and future operators of the
plant. In addition, all possible efforts must be made to mx1inimize the environmental
impact of his activities.
19.4 All required precautions for the work to proceed safely without interruption (health,
safety, and environment) during site erection must be taken into account.
19.5 Vendor shall provide any necessary recommendation for installation in order to ensure
a safe erection and operation of his equipment.
19.6 Since during pre-shut down (PSD) the revamp activities are to be taken up in a running
plant, all necessary barricading, safety related precautions, tools & tackles shall be the
Contractor’s responsibility. All the OWNER’s safety recommendations, HSE requirements
and all applicable rules & regulations shall be complied to by the Contractor.
19.7 Contractor to note that during Pre shut down hot work shall be permitted with compliance
of safety requirements / local barricading isolation. Owner’s approval for the same shall be
obtained before proceeding for hot work
19.8 All the vendor employees deputed at site, including supervisors, are required to attend
safety training in BPCL failing which they will not be accepted as supervisors
irrespective of their academic qualifications and work experience. All safety
precautions as per permit conditions and as per instructions from site in charge shall
be abided by the contractor.
19.9 It shall be the sole responsibility of the vendor for safe and proper execution of the
work. Specifying any procedure or approving any procedure by BPCL shall not relieve
the contractor from his responsibility towards fulfilling job objective.
19.10 All the BPCL's fire & safety rules and regulations or any other framed from time to time
are essentially to be followed and the vendor is liable for any lapses and penal
deduction from the bills shall be levied as per BPCL's safety financial deterrents.
breadcrumb: VDU HEATER REVAMPAT BPCL MUMBAI REFINERY > 19.0 HSE

content:
19.11 The vendor shall compulsorily follow all the requirements/instructions of BPCL's work
authorization & permit systems and gate entry procedure for labour, staff & material as
per the specific procedures & regulations.
20.0 BID PRESENTATION
20.1 The bid shall include the following technical information along with the offer:
k) Job execution philosophy including pre shutdown and shutdown activities and the
time schedule,
l) Details of the crane deployment and the site management.
m) Duly filled concession/deviation (as per attached) form concerning deviation and
exceptions if any
n) Fabrication/Delivery schedule
o) Transportation philosophy
p) List of “Main Material” Sub-Vendors
q) Supplier HSE commitment
r) List of startup & commissioning spares
s) Third party inspection agency
t) List of references (Past track record as required for BQC)
n) Other documents as listed in vendor data requirement.
21.0 DELIVERY / COMPLETION PERIOD
The Delivery/ Completion period is defined as follows:
21.1 The start date of the job will be the PO date and the PO will be valid for one year period.
21.2 The subject heater revamp is planned during the turnaround of CDU3 unit in the month
of April 2026 with an expected start date of 16 th April 2026.
21.3 The shutdown duration will be for 30 days with available engineering days of 22 for the
heater revamp job. The engineering days will govern as the time of completion for this
contract.
21.4 The pre-shutdown activity of this job is planned to start from 20 th of Feb 2026 and the
contractor should mobilise the required manpower and resources for the same.
21.5 All the material in the scope of supply of the contractor should reach the worksite latest
by 15 th Feb 2026.
22.0 PRICE REDUCTION SCHEDULE (PRS) FOR DELAY IN DELIVERY /
COMPLETION
Price reduction due to delay/slippage in delivery/completion shall be applicable at the
rate of 1 % (One percentage) of total contract cost for every day of delay or part thereof
subject to maximum of 5% (Five percentage) of total contract cost
23.0 PAYMENT TERMS
23.1 For Heater Revamp Job as per the Line Item
f. 20% against delivery of material at site
g. 20% against completion of Pre TA activities.
h. 40% against completion of jobs during TA
